description: You are an environmental technician analyzing heavy metal contamination in the Meuse river floodplain. Perform a geostatistical analysis of zinc concentrations using the 'meuse' and 'meuse.grid' datasets from the 'sp' package. Your goal is to produce a kriging interpolation map and assess model quality by fitting a variogram model to log-transformed zinc concentrations (comparing at least two models), performing Ordinary Kriging across the grid, and conducting Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV). Save the following deliverables to /home/ga/RProjects/output/: 'meuse_variogram_model.csv' (columns: model, nugget, partial_sill, range, sse), 'meuse_kriging_predictions.csv' (columns: x, y, zinc_pred, zinc_var), 'meuse_cv_results.csv' (columns: observed, predicted, residual, zscore), a 4-panel 'meuse_kriging_maps.png' showing sample locations, kriging predictions, kriging variance, and the variogram fit, and the complete 'meuse_kriging_analysis.R' script. Note that the meuse.grid object requires conversion to a SpatialPixelsDataFrame or similar for kriging.
